mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating household income and consumption demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where unemployment rises by more than 0.4 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in consumption and housing starts fails to yield positive 60-day returns in >50% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during a post-recession recovery phase reflects improving corporate capacity utilization and earnings momentum, triggering positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth yields negative 5-day forward returns in >55% of cases during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation reading exceeding 0.25 percent month-over-month triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of months where CPI inflation exceeds 0.25 percent month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive equity returns over three months.
failure_condition: Concurrent payroll growth and unemployment declines produce negative 60-day returns in >50% of observed periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate profit expectations and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive quarterly GDP growth correlates with negative 5-day forward returns in >55% of release periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent during a monetary tightening cycle signals weakening economic activity and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production drops exceeding 0.5 percent yield positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases during tightening regimes.
